steps.title: Use the weighted-mean shortcut
steps.content: Do not average $32$ and $62$ directly, because the two data sets have different sizes. Use a weighted mean: multiply each mean by the number of buildings in that set. Set A contributes $75(32)=2400$ total meters, and set B contributes $50(62)=3100$ total meters.

steps.title: Enter the table in Desmos
steps.content: A quick Desmos table/list setup is to put the counts in one list and the means in another. In Desmos, `total` adds a list, so $\frac{\operatorname{total}(n_1m_1)}{\operatorname{total}(n_1)}$ gives the combined mean.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The combined total is $2400+3100=5500$ meters across $75+50=125$ buildings, so the combined mean is $5500/125=44$. The answer is 44.
